title: Escobaria zilziana
intro: Escobaria zilziana, also known as the Zilz's pincushion cactus, is a rare and endangered species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions, such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Escobaria zilziana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
taxonomy: Escobaria zilziana is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Cactaceae. The genus Escobaria is further divided into several species, with Escobaria zilziana being one of them. As a member of the Cactaceae family, Escobaria zilziana is closely related to other species within the cactus family. It is classified within the subfamily Cactoideae, which includes many other species of cacti.
